prompt: Please do the task stated in `/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2_generation_prompts.md`, where the TASK_ID is `4a1cacc2`.
codex_response:
Rule: find the single non-background seed pixel, pick the unique nearest grid corner by Manhattan distance, and fill the full axis-aligned rectangle between that pixel and that corner with the seed color. I used the summary hint because it matched all official examples; no `notes.md` or new DSL primitive was needed.

Implemented the task package in [__init__.py](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_4a1cacc2/__init__.py#L1), [verifier.py](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_4a1cacc2/verifier.py#L4), and [generator.py](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_4a1cacc2/generator.py#L8). The generator explicitly samples even-sized `8`-background grids, a single seed in colors `4/6/9`, and rectangle extents from a chosen corner so the nearest-corner condition stays unambiguous and visually on-family.

Validation passed end to end: official examples `5/5`, generated examples `1000/1000`, and I visually checked [preview_01.png](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/artifacts/4a1cacc2/previews/preview_01.png) against the task family.
